does: This paper presents an AI red teaming agent built on the Dreadnode SDK that automates the construction and execution of adversarial workflows against AI systems. Rather than requiring operators to manually assemble attacks, transforms, and scorers from scratch, the agent accepts natural language descriptions of probing goals through a terminal interface and generates appropriate workflows across a library of 45+ attacks, 450+ transforms, and 130+ scorers. The system reduces red teaming cycles from weeks to hours by eliminating the manual workflow engineering bottleneck, enabling operators to probe multi-agent systems, multilingual targets, and multimodal systems while focusing on security objectives rather than implementation details.
connects: This work extends the established practice of AI red teaming—a critical defense mechanism for deployed systems in high-stakes domains—by addressing a key inefficiency in current approaches that rely on manual, library-specific workflow construction. It builds on prior work in adversarial robustness testing and safety evaluation of large language models by automating the trial-and-error cycle of attack selection and composition. The paper responds to the growing complexity of modern AI systems (agents, multimodal models, multilingual deployments) that outpace manual red teaming capabilities, drawing on agent-based automation and natural language interfaces to bridge the gap between security goals and technical implementation.
enables: By dramatically accelerating red teaming workflows and lowering the technical barrier to probing AI systems, this work enables more frequent and comprehensive security assessments of deployed AI systems across critical domains. It creates infrastructure for continuous vulnerability discovery in rapidly evolving agentic systems where static attack libraries quickly become outdated. The natural language interface and automated workflow generation point toward future integration with automated defense mechanisms and continuous red teaming pipelines that respond to emerging vulnerabilities in real time. This approach could enable organizations to shift from reactive, time-intensive security reviews to proactive, rapid-iteration security testing throughout the AI system lifecycle.
